title: pouzolzia rugulosa
intro: Pouzolzia rugulosa, also known as the Indian spurge, is a type of tropical plant species native to India and other parts of Asia. It is a medicinal plant valued for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Pouzolzia rugulosa has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits of reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and promoting wound healing. The plant's medicinal properties are often extracted and prepared as herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for local use in traditional medicine.
